 {"id":"bd-2mcr","title":"Interactive: /settings UI component (selector scaffold)","description":"# Goal
Create the interactive `/settings` UI component and navigation scaffolding.

# Scope
- A list/selector UI that can:
  - show available setting entries
  - navigate up/down
  - confirm selection
  - cancel and return to editor

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/settings` replaces the editor with the settings UI and restores it on exit.
- [ ] No settings are mutated yet (this is scaffolding).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:20.491176914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:20.502304118Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mcr","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ni","title":"E2E: Rust extension runtime smoke suite (sample) with verbose logs","description":"Background:
- We need an end-to-end validation that sampled extensions run in Rust with real wiring.

Steps:
- Build an E2E script that loads each sampled extension from `docs/extension-sample.json` and runs its scenarios.
- Select runtime tier **generically** based on artifact/manifest (WASM host vs PiJS JS tier); no per-extension branching.
- Capture stdout/stderr, tool outputs, and event hook logs per extension.
- Store logs as artifacts for debugging (per-extension log file).

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs: extension id, scenario id, tool/command/event type.

Acceptance:
- E2E script passes for the full sample and produces logs suitable for triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:46:59.333523573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:12:28.306938455Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-34f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3rr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2p8","title":"Extension event hooks + cancellation semantics","description":"Background:
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE defines event hooks (session_before_switch/fork/compact/etc.) that can cancel actions.
- Extension runtime must enforce ordering + cancellation and emit extension_error when handlers fail.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement event hook dispatch order and payloads for session lifecycle + tool/turn events.
2) Honor cancellation responses (e.g., session_before_switch/fork/compact) and propagate to UI/status.
3) Add tests that verify cancellation prevents side effects and errors surface correctly.
4) Add conformance fixtures for cancellation edge cases.

Acceptance:
- Event hooks run in the correct order and can veto actions.
- Cancellation is observable in UI/RPC and session state is unchanged.
- Tests + fixtures cover both success and cancel paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:45.825124669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:01:45.846001395Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p8","depends_on_id":"bd-576","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2pzp","title":"Message queue: pending-messages UI in interactive mode","description":"# Goal
Add a UI region to interactive mode that shows queued messages (steering/follow-up) while the agent is busy.

# Required Behavior
- While busy, display:
  - number of queued steering messages
  - number of queued follow-up messages
  - preview of queued text (truncate long lines)

# UX Notes
- Keep it compact; must not overwhelm the main conversation viewport.
- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User can always see what is queued and what will be sent.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:07.592964192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:38:34.090375604Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-2skp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2pzp","depends_on_id":"bd-3v08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qd","title":"Define per-extension scenario suite for capture","description":"Background:
- Each extension may expose tools, slash commands, and event hooks; we must test them consistently.

Steps:
- For each sampled extension, enumerate available features (tools/commands/events).
- Define minimal smoke scenarios + edge cases (inputs, expected outputs).
- Document any required configuration or secrets (mocked).

Output:
- A scenario specification mapped to each extension in the sample manifest.

Acceptance:
- Every extension has at least one scenario per supported feature category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:28.650419393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619339695Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619279633Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extension capture scenario suite spec","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qd","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qk","title":"Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity","description":"# Epic: Complete pi_agent_rust Port to 100% Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature/functionality coverage of the original Pi Agent (TypeScript) with:
- Complete conformance harness validating behavioral equivalence
- Comprehensive test coverage without mocks where possible
- Full integration with sibling libraries (asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust)
- Detailed E2E integration test scripts with verbose logging

## Background
pi_agent_rust is a Rust port of Pi Agent (TypeScript CLI) by Mario Zechner. The port uses
spec-first methodology: extract behavior from legacy → implement from spec → never translate
line-by-line. Currently at ~85% feature complete with all core functionality working.

## Current State (as of 2026-02-02)
- ✅ Core Types: All message types, content blocks, usage tracking
- ✅ 7 Built-in Tools: read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls (122 conformance tests)
- ✅ Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure (all streaming + tool use)
- ✅ Agent Runtime: Full loop with tool iteration and event callbacks
- ✅ Session Management: JSONL v3 format, tree structure, branching
- ✅ Interactive TUI: Elm Architecture with bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour
- ✅ CLI: Full argument parsing, subcommands, package management
- ✅ Authentication: auth.json storage, OAuth login/logout
- ✅ Compaction: Full context compaction with LLM summarization
- ✅ RPC Mode: Complete JSON protocol implementation
- ✅ Benchmarks: Truncation, SSE parsing with performance budgets

## Key Gaps Requiring Work
1. **HTTP Migration**: Providers still use reqwest; asupersync HTTP client stubbed
2. **Extensions Runtime**: Protocol defined but no WASM loader/runtime
3. **Session Index Integration**: Implemented but not wired into CLI
4. **Themes**: Not implemented
5. **Test Coverage**: Critical gaps in provider streaming, TUI, CLI, compaction

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All providers use asupersync HTTP client (tokio removed)
- [ ] Extension runtime loads and executes WASM extensions
- [ ] Session picker uses SQLite index for fast lookups
- [ ] Theme system loads and applies themes
- [ ] 95%+ test coverage without mocks for core paths
- [ ] E2E tests with real API calls (recorded/VCR style)
- [ ] All conformance fixtures pass
- [ ] Performance targets met (startup <100ms, binary <20MB)

## Workstreams (child features)
1. HTTP Migration (HIGH priority) - Blocks tokio removal
2. Test Coverage Expansion (HIGH priority) - Quality gate
3. Session Index Integration (MEDIUM priority) - UX improvement
4. Themes (LOW priority) - Nice to have
5. Extensions Runtime (tracked separately in existing beads bd-25q)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:31:24.363150202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29.025096826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":170,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cass + EXTENSIONS.md notes for port completeness:
- Extension runtime is explicitly Node/Bun-free: Tier A WASM component (default), Tier B JS compiled to QuickJS bytecode or JS→WASM, Tier C MCP process IPC.
- Connector model: pi.tool/exec/fs/http/session/ui/events hostcalls only; capability-gated, auditable, no ambient OS access.
- QuickJS event loop must drive microtasks + hostcall futures deterministically (asupersync LabRuntime-friendly).
- Protocol is versioned JSON + WIT; extension_ui_request/response + cancelable hooks are first-class and must be covered by conformance + benchmarks.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:21Z"},{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added missing Phase-9-ish beads not previously represented in open/in-progress work:
- Release engineering workstream: bd-gqtd (with versioning/changelog/CI-matrix/GH releases/docs/badges subtasks)
- Error UX workstream: bd-3am2 (hint mapping + rendering + tests)
- Rustdoc workstream: bd-14od (public API audit + doc build check)
- Troubleshooting doc: bd-3oa9 (under docs parity bd-3m7f)

Per guidance, I did not create/claim new extension-runtime work; these are non-extension polish/release/documentation gaps.","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:25:25Z"},{"id":172,"issue_id":"bd-2qk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added additional non-extension gap-tracking beads (docs/spec drift + AgentCx + property tests):

- bd-gor5 (workstream) Docs/spec drift fix (README/PLAN/FEATURE_PARITY)
  - bd-30zd README asupersync/tokio status correction
  - bd-jw5q PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md reconcile checklist → beads
  - bd-33jg FEATURE_PARITY.md refresh statuses + correct bead refs (themes/conformance)
  - bd-24rp AGENTS.md remove stale tokio-migration statements

- bd-3i7u asupersync: define AgentCx capability wrapper (child of bd-20c)

- bd-2lr3 (workstream) Property-based + fuzz testing for core parsers (child of bd-26s)
  - bd-30hr proptest truncation invariants
  - bd-2sl9 proptest SSE parser chunk-boundary robustness
  - bd-2127 proptest session JSONL roundtrip + tree invariants

Intent: eliminate doc drift, make the remaining live plan fully represented in Beads, and add extra safety nets (property tests) beyond legacy parity.","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:05:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qrp","title":"Settings: UI toggles (quietStartup, collapseChangelog, hideThinkingBlock, cursor, padding)","description":"# Goal
Expose common UI/display settings in `/settings` and ensure they take effect.

# Settings (legacy docs/settings.md)
- `quietStartup` (hide startup header)
- `collapseChangelog` (condense changelog)
- `hideThinkingBlock`
- `showHardwareCursor`
- `doubleEscapeAction` (tree|fork)
- `editorPaddingX`
- `autocompleteMaxVisible`

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toggles can be changed via `/settings`.
- [ ] Changes persist.
- [ ] Interactive UI responds immediately where feasible.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:44:56.637633490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:45:22.665988486Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-1cd5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-2mcr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qrp","depends_on_id":"bd-axuu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-btq","title":"Extensions: Node/Bun-free runtime (PiJS) — prove superior path","description":"# Goal
Deliver a Node/Bun-free extension runtime (PiJS) that is **more secure, more deterministic, and faster** than legacy Node/Bun extensions, while ensuring **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified**.

- “Unmodified” = no manual edits to extension source. Normal install/build steps and the **extc compile/rewrite pipeline** are allowed as part of the runtime.

# Background / Motivation
- Mario’s critique is correct: QuickJS lacks Node/Bun OS wrappers and event loop. We replace that with a **minimal, capability-gated PiJS runtime + deterministic event loop**.
- The win condition is not “be Node”; the win condition is: **run the real extensions**, with a smaller surface, better security posture, and better observability.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in runtime, shims, or compiler rewrites.
- All behavior differences must be attributable to: artifact bytes, manifest/capabilities, policy mode, and deterministic runtime config.

# Strategy (PiJS)
1) **Connector-first architecture** — all I/O is explicit `host_call` into the connector dispatcher; no ambient OS APIs.
2) **Deterministic event loop** — microtasks, timers, hostcall promises driven by asupersync with strict ordering rules.
3) **Hybrid WASM approach (assume QuickJS has no WebAssembly)**
   - Tier A: WASM components via the WASM host (bd-3d1/bd-nom).
   - Tier B: JS compatibility via QuickJS **bytecode**.
   - For JS-tier bundles that expect `globalThis.WebAssembly`: provide a **PiWasm bridge** backed by wasmtime (bd-1ry).
4) **Node-compat shims (surgical, sample-driven)**
   - Implement only the Node core APIs actually required by the pinned sample set.
   - Provide `node:*` shims and globals (`process`, `Buffer`, etc.) implemented on top of connectors with capability enforcement (bd-3d0, bd-2sr).
5) **Compatibility tooling** — extc rewrite + compat scanner. Unsupported APIs rejected with actionable diagnostics and evidence ledgers.
6) **Safety + performance proof** — explicit capabilities, resource limits, benchmarks with reproducible outputs.
7) **Developer UX** — structured logs + trace viewer + remediation hints.
8) **Testing** — comprehensive unit, conformance, and E2E scripts with detailed logging artifacts.

# Success Criteria (hard)
- **16/16 pinned sample extensions** run end-to-end with **no manual source edits**.
- Deterministic ordering across repeated deterministic runs (trace equality where expected).
- Capability policy enforced for every hostcall with structured logs.
- Benchmarks meet or beat budgets, and regressions are catchable.
- Evidence binder (docs + logs + benchmarks) is reproducible.

# Non-Goals
- Full Node/Bun parity beyond what is required for the pinned sample set.
- Implicit global OS access.

# Notes for Future Self
This is the umbrella for the **"prove them wrong"** path. Any new capability surface must be: capability-gated, deterministic under test, and covered by unit + E2E tests with structured logs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:18:20.201357583Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:00:29.794154307Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-193","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1fg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1nq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1pb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1ry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1uj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-1uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-2dd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-2hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-btq","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"20  {"id":"bd-cru","title":"Keybindings: define action catalog + default bindings","description":"# Goal
Create the authoritative list of interactive **actions** and their **default keybindings**, matching legacy Pi Agent.

# Background
Legacy action list and defaults are documented in:
- `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/docs/keybindings.md`

This task is the foundation for parsing config overrides and routing key events.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Define an `AppAction`-like enum (or equivalent) with all actions from the legacy docs:
  - Cursor movement
  - Deletion
  - Text input (`newLine`, `submit`, `tab`)
  - Kill ring (`yank`, `yankPop`, `undo`) — if not implemented yet, still define actions for future parity
  - Clipboard (`copy`, `pasteImage`)
  - Application (`interrupt`, `clear`, `exit`, `suspend`, `externalEditor`)
  - Session (`newSession`, `tree`, `fork`)
  - Models & thinking (`selectModel`, `cycleModelForward/backward`, `cycleThinkingLevel`)
  - Display (`expandTools`, `toggleThinking`)
  - Message queue (`followUp`, `dequeue`)
  - Selection lists/pickers
  - Session picker actions

- Define the default bindings for each action (keys list) matching the legacy doc.

# Implementation Notes
- Keep the action IDs stable (strings or serde names) so they round-trip with JSON config.
- Prefer a single source of truth for:
  - action id (machine)
  - display name (human)
  - default keys
  - category (for `/hotkeys` grouping)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Action list matches legacy (no missing actions).
- [ ] Default bindings match legacy defaults.
- [ ] There is a way to iterate actions (for `/hotkeys` rendering).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:36:08.298061995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:08.671004588Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:40:08.670942141Z","close_reason":"Created keybindings.rs with full action catalog (48 actions in 12 categories), default bindings matching legacy, JSON serialization support. Module compiles but tests blocked by unrelated errors in other modules being modified by other agents.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cru","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-dj27","title":"Release: versioning + tag strategy (pre-1.0 → 1.0)","description":"# Goal
Define the single source of truth for versioning and tags so releases are repeatable and humans don’t have to remember tribal knowledge.

# Background
- This repo ships a `pi` binary and also exposes a library crate (`pi`).
- Publishing to crates.io and publishing GitHub Releases both need a consistent version scheme.

# Decisions to Make (write down explicitly)
1) **When do we call it 1.0?**
   - What is the minimum feature set / stability bar?
2) **Version coupling:**
   - Are `pi_agent_rust` crate version, `pi` binary `--version`, and any published sibling crates (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust) versioned together or independently?
3) **Tag format:**
   - e.g. `vX.Y.Z` tags for repo releases.
4) **Changelog source:**
   - Prefer generating `CHANGELOG.md` from closed beads via `br changelog` (see sibling task).

# Output / Deliverables
- A short doc section (can live in `docs/releasing.md`) describing:
  - version bump procedure
  - tag naming
  - how to cut a patch release
  - what is required before tagging (quality gates + CI green)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A human can follow the written steps and create a release without guessing.
- [ ] Version/tag policy is consistent with existing `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and crate metadata.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:20:59.131169345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:21:18.585234269Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dj27","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-dj27","title":"Release: versioning + tag strategy (pre-1.0 → 1.0)","description":"# Goal
Define the single source of truth for versioning and tags so releases are repeatable and humans don’t have to remember tribal knowledge.

# Background
- This repo ships a `pi` binary and also exposes a library crate (`pi`).
- Publishing to crates.io and publishing GitHub Releases both need a consistent version scheme.

# Decisions to Make (write down explicitly)
1) **When do we call it 1.0?**
   - What is the minimum feature set / stability bar?
2) **Version coupling:**
   - Are `pi_agent_rust` crate version, `pi` binary `--version`, and any published sibling crates (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust) versioned together or independently?
3) **Tag format:**
   - e.g. `vX.Y.Z` tags for repo releases.
4) **Changelog source:**
   - Prefer generating `CHANGELOG.md` from closed beads via `br changelog` (see sibling task).

# Output / Deliverables
- A short doc section (can live in `docs/releasing.md`) describing:
  - version bump procedure
  - tag naming
  - how to cut a patch release
  - what is required before tagging (quality gates + CI green)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A human can follow the written steps and create a release without guessing.
- [ ] Version/tag policy is consistent with existing `.github/workflows/publish.yml` and crate metadata.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:20:59.131169345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T00:42:50.759240204Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T00:42:50.759173199Z","close_reason":"Added docs/releasing.md with version/tag policy + release steps (commit 99b00e4)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dj27","depends_on_id":"bd-gqtd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-doi","title":"Coverage tooling + CI gating (llvm-cov)","description":"Goal:
- Add repeatable coverage tooling (llvm-cov) and CI gating that enforces agreed coverage thresholds and the no-mock policy.

Why this matters:
- Coverage is only meaningful if it reflects real behavior (no mocks) and is stable in CI.
- We need artifacts + logs to debug coverage regressions quickly.

Scope:
- Choose tool (cargo llvm-cov preferred) and pin exact command(s).
- Add local + CI commands for coverage runs (text + optional HTML).
- Define thresholds (overall + per-module if feasible) based on bd-351 output.
- Force VCR playback in CI (no network) and ensure secrets are redacted.
- Emit detailed logging for coverage runs (command, env, duration, artifacts).

Deliverables:
- Documented coverage command in README/CONFORMANCE.md or docs/testing.
- CI step that fails when coverage drops below threshold.
- Coverage artifacts (summary + HTML) and logs stored as CI artifacts.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Running the coverage command locally produces a report without network calls.
- CI fails on threshold breach and passes with current baseline.
- Logs clearly show command, env (redacted), duration, artifact paths, and summary.
- No mocks/fakes are used to raise coverage (enforced by policy check).

Dependencies:
- Uses bd-351 coverage matrix for thresholds/scope.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T04:58:14.468787870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T08:37:33.106372716Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T08:37:33.106312804Z","close_reason":"Coverage baseline captured; CI gate set to 30%","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-doi","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-doi","depends_on_id":"bd-351","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-enwy","title":"Interactive: collapse/expand tool output (expandTools)","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy tool output collapse/expand.

# Required Behavior
- Default: tool calls/results are visible.
- `expandTools` action (legacy default Ctrl+O) toggles collapse state.
- When collapsed:
  - show a single-line summary per tool call/result with an expand hint.

# Dependencies
- Requires structured tool items (`bd-385a`).
- Requires keybinding router (`bd-gze`) to map Ctrl+O.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ctrl+O toggles tool output visibility.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:52:24.034150558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:52:54.006402705Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-enwy","depends_on_id":"bd-217r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-enwy","depends_on_id":"bd-385a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-enwy","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fww","title":"Unit tests: model message/content serialization + invariants","description":"# Goal
Cover `src/model.rs` with unit tests for message/content serialization, invariants, and round-trips.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Round-trip JSON for `Message` variants (user/assistant/tool_result).
- `ContentBlock` variants (text/image/tool_call/tool_result) including empty/edge cases.
- `ThinkingLevel`, `StopReason`, `ToolCall`/`ToolResult` schema invariants.
- `Usage` aggregation/defaults and serialization.
- `StreamEvent` encoding/decoding for start/delta/done events.

# No‑Mock Rule
- Use real serde JSON round-trips and real structs only.

# Acceptance
- 20+ focused unit tests.
- Round-trip tests cover all enums and edge cases.
- Tests assert invariants and user-facing error messages.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:25:36.713206016Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:19:42.821407748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fww","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fww","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"bd-fww","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Cover JSON round-trips for User/Assistant/ToolResult messages with multiple ContentBlock sequences.
- Include image blocks (base64, mime) and tool_call/tool_result payloads.
- Validate enum string representations (thinking levels, stop reasons) match provider expectations.
- Add a fixture for StreamEvent::Delta vs ::Done ordering invariants.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:49Z"},{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-fww","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: 48 comprehensive tests in tests/model_serialization.rs covering all Message variants, ContentBlock types, ThinkingLevel, StopReason, Usage, and edge cases. Tests pass. Bead ready to close once parent bd-102 is resolved.","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:19:42Z"}]}
